message:
fix(observability): reject duplicate finding IDs, share min_level across clones, fix JSON field namespace collisions

Analyzer plugin registry (analyzer_plugin.rs):
- Extract normalize_plugin_findings() to sort findings deterministically
  and detect duplicate finding_id values. Duplicate IDs are rejected as
  a ContractViolation with error_code "duplicate_finding_id", preventing
  downstream consumers from silently merging or losing findings.
- Extend the pack report's aggregated_findings sort to break ties on
  severity, summary, and confidence_bps (previously only plugin_id and
  finding_id), eliminating non-determinism in report output.
- Added test: run_pack_rejects_duplicate_finding_ids_as_contract_violation.

Log collector (collector.rs):
- Move min_level from LogCollector (outside the Arc<Mutex>) into
  CollectorInner (inside the Mutex). Previously, cloning a collector
  and calling with_min_level on the clone would silently diverge —
  the clone would filter at the new level while the original kept the
  old one, even though they share the same entry buffer. Now both
  clones see the same min_level because it lives behind the shared lock.
- with_min_level now takes self (not mut self) since it locks internally.
- Added test: clone shares min_level configuration.

Log entry JSON serialization (entry.rs):
- Fix recursive namespace collision: a user field named "field.message"
  was previously emitted as "field.message" in JSON, colliding with the
  namespaced form of the reserved "message" key. Now json_field_key()
  detects any chain of "field." prefixes that resolves to a reserved
  name (e.g., "field.field.message" -> "field.message" -> "message")
  and adds one more "field." prefix to guarantee uniqueness.
- Extract FIELD_NAMESPACE_PREFIX constant for clarity.
- Added test: json_reserved_alias_family_remains_collision_free verifying
  that "message", "field.message", "field.field.message" all serialize
  to distinct JSON keys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
